title: ECB Macroprudential Bulletin Issue 32 (November 2025) - EU-Wide Stress Test Extensions
summary: This source is a table of contents/listing page for ECB Macroprudential Bulletin Issue 32 (November 2025), presenting article titles and brief descriptions on macroprudential topics. The content covers extensions to the 2025 EU-wide stress test, including bank deleveraging dynamics, contagion risk integration, climate risk assessment, and scenario severity frameworks. No specific quantitative data, findings, or market-moving figures are provided in this source.
key_points: 2025 EU-wide stress test extensions explored for macroprudential policy insights
Articles examine bank deleveraging and capital buffer release impacts on balance sheets and real economy
System-wide contagion analysis incorporates investment funds, insurance corporations, and banks under EBA adverse scenario
Climate risk integration combines EBA adverse scenario with NGFS scenarios for transition and physical risk assessment
Framework introduced for assessing severity of EU-wide stress test adverse scenarios
New approach proposed for deriving target 'positive neutral' CCyB rate using stress test data
why_it_matters: This listing page provides no standalone research value. Individual article pages should be crawled separately for substantive content on macroprudential stress testing methodology, systemic risk assessment, and countercyclical capital buffer policy calibration.
macro_context: ECB macroprudential research on stress test methodology and systemic risk assessment provides background for understanding potential bank sector vulnerabilities. However, this listing page does not contain the actual research findings - only references to articles that should be crawled individually.
